title: Accessibility + bias/sensitivity review checklist
context: Assessment content can exclude disabled learners (missing alt text, color-only cues, inaccessible math) and encode stereotypes or cultural biases that harm the learners OER is meant to serve. Both accessibility and bias/sensitivity review are mandatory, non-skippable gates before any bank ships. A formal checklist is required so that reviews are consistent, auditable, and attached as committed artifacts. This checklist also operationalizes the automated lint targets for the validator (quiz-bank-open-validator-006).
objective: Produce a committed accessibility and bias/sensitivity review checklist covering the manual and automated checks required before any bank is considered done.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessibility checklist covers: alt text present for all images and non-text assets; no color-only visual cues that convey answer information; math rendered in a screen-reader-compatible format (MathML with LaTeX source retained; aria/MathJax fallback path specified); plain language (no construct-irrelevant complexity); checklist maps to the accessibility fields in the canonical item model.

Bias/sensitivity checklist covers: no stereotyped or exclusionary assumptions (culture, region, gender, disability, socioeconomic); no trick wording or construct-irrelevant difficulty; synthetic, bias-screened names and contexts; checklist is usable by a non-SME reviewer.
Each checklist item specifies whether it is automated (CI lint) or human review, and whether a failure is blocking.
Checklist produces a committed PASS/ISSUES artifact per bank.
Document is CC-BY-4.0 licensed; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: Haladyna/Rodriguez item-writing guidelines (distractor and stem quality)
output: A committed accessibility and bias/sensitivity review checklist with PASS/ISSUES artifact format, ready for use by the validator, reviewers, and all per-bank authoring tasks.
